name: Aluminum
summary: Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **STALENESS CORRECTION: the prior (May 19) summary read aluminum as soft/falling toward $3,400 on China-demand skepticism — that is OUT OF DATE. ALUMINUM IS NOW AT A 4-YEAR HIGH ~$3,640-3,680 (+0.4% 1D, +2.6% MoM, +17.4% 3M). The yfinance -13% 1D print is the recurring LME-vintage / stale-comparator ARTIFACT (LME closed/continuous-contract roll) — DISCARD it, exactly as on May 19-20.** DUAL DRIVER (roughly 50/50): (a) CRISIS — the Mideast naval blockade halts Gulf exports = ~9% of global aluminum supply offline (EGA Al Taweelah, ALBA, Borouge); (b) NON-CRISIS SUPPLY SHOCK — Guinea announced bauxite export controls starting June (bauxite is the upstream feedstock for alumina→aluminum). The Guinea overlay is the key intelligence: part of the strength is now structural and PERSISTS even on a Gulf/Hormuz deal — a deal removes the ~50% crisis premium but NOT the Guinea bauxite-control leg. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline (~12-month restart) remains the structural floor. PRIOR (May 19): Day 80 (Monday). **C8 ALUMINUM ~FLAT ACTUAL MONDAY ($3,503.50 vs Friday $3,511 = ~-0.2%); yfinance -7.6% IS STALE-COMPARATOR ARTIFACT (same artifact flagged for WTI).** Friday May 15 move was -9.8% premium unwind on Trump-Xi summit Hormuz-reopen optics + 4-year-high long-positioning blow-out + EGA Hormuz-premium unwind = the cycle-level event; Monday is consolidation. Underlying drivers per LME / Discovery Alert / westmetall: weak early-session spot purchasing sentiment + profit-taking after Q1 rally; softening industrial output indicators in major consuming regions Q2 2026; China social inventory at 5-year period high (inventory buildup cycle not ended); LME inventories fell another 0.72% but Chinese hubs (Wuxi) active destocking cushioned downside. **Assessment**: Friday's -9.8% was position-unwind on Trump-Xi optics; Monday's consolidation says market is NOT extrapolating Hormuz reopening despite Trump rhetoric. **If Tuesday produces strike order, aluminum snaps back +3-5% to ~$3,680; if Tuesday produces back-off, aluminum continues weak toward $3,400.** The asymmetry is real — physical Hormuz remains constrained even with Trump-Xi joint-statement language. **EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor.** **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICE IS FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSE $3,511/mt per markets dossier Section A** (-9.8% 1D, -3.7% 1M, +18.2% 3M vs pre-war ~$2,650). Per markets dossier C6: **EGA Hormuz-premium 4-year-high long blown out** + Trump-Xi joint statement Hormuz language + UAE-side rerouting (Modi-MBZ MoUs include ADNOC SPR + Indian crude at Fujairah outside Hormuz) = traders priced reduced near-term physical disruption. **Assessment: POSITIONING UNWIND, NOT thesis-falsification.** Underlying disruption (EGA still offline, ALBA suspended, ~9% of global aluminum flow tied to Hormuz) is structurally intact. **If NYT preparation report operationalizes Monday, aluminum snaps back violently.** Crisis vs non-crisis split: ~80% crisis-trade-unwind / ~20% China-demand-skepticism. **Monday May 18 binary**: if NYT-priced gap-up in Brent on Monday open, aluminum re-prices Hormuz tail-risk premium back in. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor. **PRIOR (May 16):** LME ALUMINUM $3,791.50/mt -2.6% 1D / +3.9% 1M / +27.6% 3M — second-leg metals data**. Extending Thursday's -8.9% but at a more controlled pace; tariff overhang (50% Trump April clarification) + 4-yr-high long unwind continues; ~40% crisis-residual, ~60% non-crisis (tariff + China property + global industrial moderation). **The metals selloff Friday is now INFLATION-DRIVEN rather than geopolitically-driven** (markets dossier B3) — a different structural mechanism that points to a different recovery path. **The graph's 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift / below $4,400 sustained 5+ sessions = second-leg premium unwind' trigger added yesterday is tracking the bear-case at Day 1 of 5-session window** (gold $4,564, silver $77.16). **Silver -10.6% one-day cut Fortune intraday; -9.1% close — Goldman attribution: rising yields drove selloff + dual identity (monetary + industrial) made it hardest hit on rates shock + risk-off + dollar-bid combo; gold/silver ratio widened to 58.9.** Copper -4.8% (China stimulus skepticism + Trump-Xi tariff softening absence + risk-off cascade); Platinum -4.9% (precious-industrial complex hit on real-yield repricing + Trump-Xi disappointment); Palladium -2.3% (auto-demand weakness; industrial complex unwind). **EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor.** PRIOR (May 15): **C2 LME ALUMINUM -8.9% TO $3,545.75/mt — GEOPOLITICAL PREMIUM UNWIND, BIGGEST 1D DROP IN COMMODITY COMPLEX**. Primary chain (~95% crisis-linked): Aluminum had just hit four-year high $3,729.50 cash on May 13 on Hormuz/EGA supply premium (EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline, ~12-month restart); Trump-Xi joint statement May 14-15 'Strait of Hormuz must remain open' + China rare-earth export pause + tariff truce extension; market read this as the beginning of the war-discount unwind that Morgan Stanley had flagged; most-crowded long in base metals (cash +19.3% 3M, LME stocks at multi-year lows ~348-358kt); position liquidation violent off stretched long; SHFE roughly flat May 14 = confirms this is LME/Western unwind, not Shanghai-led. **Secondary contributor (~0.4%)**: No new supply news. No smelter restart, no Chinese export surge, no LME stockpile release. Physical supply is unchanged. **Assessment**: PURE REPRICING OF GEOPOLITICAL RISK PREMIUM following Trump-Xi Hormuz commitment. **Reversibility: HIGH for sentiment component; physical floor intact (EGA still offline)**. Any ceasefire violation, EGA restart slippage, or rare-earth/tariff backsliding re-inflates the premium quickly. Today's move is sentiment, not supply. **NEW TRIGGER PROPOSED**: aluminum sustains below $3,400 5+ sessions = second leg of unwind active. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline ~12-month restart remains the structural floor. **B/C3 LME ALUMINUM $3,624.50/mt (-5.6% 1D, +0.3% 1M, +22.0% 3M) — BIGGEST 1D DROP IN COMMODITY COMPLEX TODAY.** Drivers (~50% crisis, ~50% macro/demand): Position unwind + Discovery Alert '$3,559/t May 8 → $3,624 May 14' suggests cycle-low rebound got monetised; demand caution (Q2 industrial output softening) + USD bid. Gulf-supply premium UNWIND on Trump-Xi 'trade-not-war' opening + crisis-trade unwind across complex (Lockheed -15% MoM, CF -3.8%, Cheniere -2.0%, RBOB -4.5%, Frontline -3.5%). Per markets dossier B: 'positions long China-reflation (Cu +2.5%/Ag +2.6% prior session), short war-premium-supply (Al)' — today both retraced. **Copper $6.58/lb (-0.9% 1D, +8.3% 1M)** = trade-not-war narrative bid earlier retraced today; gold/silver/copper all mildly weaker = paper-physical bifurcation in metals complex reads as 'partial de-escalation pricing.' Gulf production damage unresolved (Borouge UAE, ALBA Bahrain, EGA Al Taweelah) but already in price. **Assessment: aluminum cleanest counter-signal to crisis premium re-rating — if war is truly tightening industrial-commodity supply broadly, aluminum should rally with copper. Instead BOTH retracing on Trump-Xi summit-pricing.** PRIOR (May 13): Aluminum had run to ~$3,600/mt 4-year high; current $3,510 = -2.5% from peak. **CANCELLED-WARRANT DECLINE -7% TO 26,500t SIGNALS REDUCED NEAR-TERM PHYSICAL DEMAND** — inventory queue shrinking, not deepening. Persian Gulf ~9% of global aluminum output — meaningful but not dominant; Gulf supply concerns ALREADY priced in +44% YoY level. **ASSESSMENT (markets dossier C8): Aluminum is cleanest counter-signal to fertilizer rally. If war were truly tightening industrial-commodity supply broadly, aluminum should rally with copper (+8.1% 1M, +11.9% 3M). Instead, aluminum showing DEMAND-SIDE SOFTNESS — likely China property + global industrial moderation.** **This is a signal that NOT ALL CRISIS-LINKED MOVES ARE CRISIS-DRIVEN; the global industrial cycle has its own tempo.** Watch for confirmation: if copper rolls over while gold stays muted, inflation/flow regime is shifting toward outright demand-softness regime — bad for cyclicals across the board. China May trade-data tape weakness (USD/CNY 6.79, Shanghai Composite -0.4%, property-sector weakness) contributes. Gulf production damage unresolved (Borouge UAE, ALBA Bahrain, EGA Al Taweelah) but already in price; ~10% crisis-residual, ~90% non-crisis demand-softness. Per markets.md D6: aluminum down -2.7% Apr 29 on profit-taking after April rally; Brent +6-8% to $118-120 should normally support Gulf-aluminum-supply-disruption thesis but profit-taking dominated. LME inventories contracted 0.58% to 381,050 tonnes — supply tight. Trade-policy normalization expectations. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since Apr 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk; physical-damage repair/restart 12-18 months structural. Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First reinforces structural thesis but day's tape was positioning-driven, not fundamentals. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): INFO-OPS-ADJACENT DIVERGENCE FLAGGED: Monday attribution to 'Iranian authorities stated that commercial vessels are now free to navigate the chokepoint following ongoing talks with the US' (Trading Economics/Mining.com) is OPPOSITE of the OPERATIONAL reality — 16 vessels Monday vs 138 baseline = 88% suppression; Sunday zero-transit. Someone trading aluminum read the soft Iran DIPLOMATIC quote and ignored AIS data. Also profit-taking after +11.5% 1M and $3,670 four-year high earlier in April + China export-increase expectations. Aluminum now CHEAP relative to operational reality if Wednesday ceasefire lapses — +$200-300/mt catch-down in 48-72hr. Gulf production damage unresolved: Borouge (UAE) shut since April 5; ALBA (Bahrain) 19% offline; EGA (UAE) Al Taweelah damage unrepaired. ~9% of global aluminum capacity at risk. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

recent_signals.headline: **ALUMINUM AT A 4-YEAR HIGH ~$3,640-3,680 (+0.4% 1D, +2.6% MoM, +17.4% 3M) — DUAL DRIVER: GULF NAVAL BLOCKADE (~9% OF GLOBAL SUPPLY OFFLINE) + GUINEA BAUXITE EXPORT CONTROLS FROM JUNE. yfinance -13% 1D IS THE RECURRING LME-VINTAGE ARTIFACT — DISCARD.** (1) **Staleness correction**: the prior May 19 read (soft, falling toward $3,400 on China-demand skepticism) is OUT OF DATE — aluminum is at a 4-year high. (2) **The yfinance -13% 1D is a data artifact**: same LME-vintage / stale-comparator / continuous-contract-roll mechanism flagged May 19-20 (LME closed/stale comparator divides into a fresh print). The real 1D move is ~+0.4%. (3) **DUAL DRIVER (~50/50)**: (a) CRISIS — the Mideast naval blockade halts Gulf exports, taking ~9% of global aluminum supply offline (EGA Al Taweelah 1.6Mt, ALBA, Borouge unrepaired); (b) NON-CRISIS SUPPLY SHOCK — Guinea announced bauxite export controls starting June, hitting the upstream feedstock for alumina→aluminum. (4) **The key intelligence**: the Guinea overlay means part of the strength is structural and PERSISTS even on a Gulf/Hormuz deal — a deal removes the ~50% crisis premium but NOT the Guinea bauxite-control leg. EGA Al Taweelah 1.6 Mt offline (~12-month restart) remains the structural floor.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-28 A/C6 (aluminum ~$3,640-3,680 4-year high, +0.4% 1D / +2.6% MoM / +17.4% 3M; dual driver Gulf blockade ~9% supply offline + Guinea bauxite export controls from June; yfinance -13% = LME-vintage artifact, discard)
web (Guinea bauxite export controls starting June; Gulf aluminum supply ~9% of global offline)
recent_signals.verification: aluminum ~$3,640-3,680 4-year high / +0.4% 1D / +2.6% MoM / +17.4% 3M = CONFIRMED (operative/web-verified); yfinance -13% 1D = DATA ARTIFACT (LME-vintage / stale-comparator, same mechanism as May 19-20); Gulf blockade ~9% of global supply offline = REPORTED; Guinea bauxite export controls from June = REPORTED; dual-driver ~50/50 split + 'persists even on a Gulf deal' = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (markets dossier C6).

recent_signals.headline: **ALUMINUM yfinance ALI=F -8.4% IS A DATA ARTIFACT — LIKELY -1% TO -2% REAL MOVE.** May 20, 2026 = third Wednesday = LME monthly prompt date; May 19 is when continuous-contract feeds (yfinance ALI=F) roll and reprice, producing phantom gaps when the stale prior comparator (last-trade days earlier near ~$3,850) divides into a fresh $3,524 print. Trading Economics shows May 18 aluminum at $3,561.65 (-0.07% d/d); LME 3M futures $3,573.85. Broader May pullback was -2.01% globally on China demand softness. **Fundamentals unchanged**: 2026 deficit ~140kt; LME stocks -0.72%; China 45Mt cap intact. Strait of Hormuz Closure Deepens Global Aluminum Supply Crunch (Mexico Business analysis) — structural bullishness intact. **Editor instruction**: Do NOT report -8.4% in the brief. Use TradingEconomics or westmetall for the real print; flag the yfinance ALI=F series as suspect for the May 19-20 window.
recent_signals.verification: yfinance -8.4% = DATA ARTIFACT (CONFIRMED via three corroborating series); real 1D move ~-1% to -2% = REPORTED; LME prompt-date contract roll mechanism = CONFIRMED structural.

5 signals. Aluminum LME at $3,422/mt (-0 | Gulf aluminum exposure unchanged: ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) remain in the cumulative damage envelope | Day 50
